Article Title: Poll vault: Portuguese village in WB sees minimal SIR impact
Article Text: In Mirpur village in WB’s East Midnapore, SIR has not been unpleasant, with only two of 824 voters struck off (a Hindu & a Muslim) a marked deviation from elsewhere in WB. Residents with surnames like Nunes, Rozario and D’Cruz trace their roots to Portuguese mercenaries who settled here after 1742. Today, Christians, Hindus and Muslims celebrate Eid, Puja & Christmas together.

Article Title: Narendra Modi: ‘They will never be forgotten’: PM Modi pays tribute to Pahalgam victims on first anniversary
Article Text:
File photo

Watch Indian Army Sends Strong Warning to Terror Groups Ahead of Pahalgam Terror Attack Anniversary Indian Army Sends Strong Warning to Terror Groups Ahead of Pahalgam Terror Attack Anniversary

Army reiterates ‘justice will be served’

Attack and India's response

NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Wednesday paid tribute to the victims of the Pahalgam terror attack , marking its anniversary with a strong message of unity and resolve against terrorism.In a post on X, PM Modi said, “Remembering the innocent lives lost in the gruesome Pahalgam terror attack on this day last year. They will never be forgotten. My thoughts are also with the bereaved families as they cope with this loss. ”He added, “As a nation, we stand united in grief and resolve. India will never bow to any form of terror. The heinous designs of terrorists will never succeed.”Echoing the Prime Minister’s message, the Indian Army also marked the anniversary with a firm warning. In a post on X, it said, “For acts against #India, the response is assured. Justice will be served. Always.”The Army shared a graphic highlighting “Operation Mahadev,” stating “It was only a matter of time,” and detailing how “300 sq km treacherous terrain” was scanned over “93 days and nights of relentless search,” leading to the neutralisation of “3 perpetrators.”It added that “Operation Sindoor continues.”The April 22, 2025 attack in Jammu and Kashmir’s Pahalgam had killed 26 civilians.The victims were segregated by terrorists based on religion before being shot in front of their families, sparking widespread outrage.India responded with “Operation Sindoor” on May 7, 2025, targeting terror infrastructure across the border. Nine major terror launchpads linked to Lashkar-e-Taiba, Jaish-e-Mohammed and Hizbul Mujahideen were destroyed, and over 100 terrorists were eliminated.Pakistan’s retaliation led to a brief four-day conflict before a ceasefire was reached on May 10, after Islamabad’s request.In the aftermath, Indian armed forces launched “Operation Mahadev” and tracked down and eliminated the three attackers involved in the Pahalgam killings.
Article Summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi paid tribute to the victims of the Pahalgam terror attack. Indian Army also marked the anniversary with a firm warning. In a post on X, it said, “For acts against #India, the response is assured. Justice will be served. Always”

Article Title: Justice Yashwant Verma: Evidence enough to nail Justice Varma: Probe panel counsel
Article Text:
.

Watch Justice Yashwant Varma Resigns Amid Cash Row — Why He Stepped Down On Day Of Impeachment Defence Justice Yashwant Varma Resigns Amid Cash Row — Why He Stepped Down On Day Of Impeachment Defence

NEW DELHI: Advocates assisting the Justice Aravind Kumar Committee, inquiring into the discovery of cash at Justice Yashwant Varma’s residence in March last year, on Tuesday, said the evidence produced during the proceedings was enough to substantiate the charge, which was one of the grounds cited in the removal motion against him.In a communication to the committee, senior advocates Rajkumar Bhaskar Thakare and Aishwarya Bhati, who were appointed under Judges Inquiry Committee Act to assist the panel, termed Justice Varma’s letter on April 9 withdrawing from the proceedings as an “attempt to construct a narrative of procedural unfairness where none exists, made at the stage when the judge (Varma) was called upon to answer the charges on merits, knowing full well that tendering the resignation would bring an end to these proceedings. ”The two senior advocates said the evidence substantiated the three charges: discovery and possession of unexplained Indian currency within official premises; failing to preserve and causing interference with the material evidence; and furnishing evasive and misleading explanations/statements.They told the committee that it should not let Justice Varma’s unfounded allegations go unanswered, as he had never complained of any procedural irregularity till he decided to withdraw from the proceedings, especially when he was required to rebut the evidence.The committee ought not to countenance such a move as a genuine complaint, they added.Justice Varma had said he was being asked to answer the unanswerable question: where did the money come from? Thakare and Bhati said Justice Varma was best placed to answer how wads of unaccounted for cash came to be placed in a room on his official bungalow premises in the heart of the Lutyens zone area of New Delhi.Terming Justice Varma’s allegation that “witnesses were deliberately dropped in a pattern designed to suppress evidence favourable to him” as misconceived and contemptuous, the advocates said the decision not to examine certain witnesses was taken as the documentary and oral evidence already on record was sufficient to prove the charges.If Justice Varma genuinely felt there was no evidence to prove the charges, then he should have persisted with the inquiry proceedings to get a clean chit, they said.Thakare and Bhati said Justice Varma did not dispute the following: presence of cash in the storeroom of his official residence, removal of burnt/half-burnt wads of cash from the storeroom after first responders left the premises on the night of March 14-15 last year, presence of his family members and personal staff during the firefighting operation, and control of the premises with him or his family members.
Article Summary: Advocates assisting the Justice Aravind Kumar Committee, inquiring into the discovery of cash at Justice Yashwant Varma’s residence in March last year, on Tuesday said the evidence produced during the proceedings was enough to substantiate the charge. Justice Varma had said he was being asked to answer the unanswerable question: where did the money come from?

Article Title: Luka Doncic: “Not the personality for all-time greats”: Luka Doncic gets ‘lazy’ NBA player tag amid absence from Lakers’ roster
Article Text:
Luka Doncic. Image via: Harry How/ Getty Images

Luka Doncic faces harsh criticism from Eddie Johnson after being dismissed from the MVP race



Luka Doncic. Image via: Alonzo Adams-Imagn Images

What does Eddie Johnson's criticism mean for Luka Doncic?



Luka Doncic. Image via: Mark J. Rebilas-Imagn Images

Luka Doncic faces harsh criticism at a time when his talent remains unquestioned, but his standing in the MVP race has slipped again. The Los Angeles Lakers star is putting up elite numbers, yet he finds himself outside the final shortlist. That gap between production and recognition has opened the door for sharper scrutiny, and this time it came from former NBA guard Eddie Johnson.Injuries have disrupted Doncic’s rhythm, and his absence from key stretches has only added to the noise. While peers like Nikola Jokic and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander continue to build complete seasons, Doncic’s campaign has been uneven. The numbers still shine, but the conversation around him has shifted, focusing less on brilliance and more on what might be missing.Eddie Johnson’s assessment cuts deeper than a typical critique. He praised Doncic’s skill, calling him nearly impossible to defend, but quickly turned to what he sees as a pattern holding the guard back.“Luka Doncic is the most unguardable player in the NBA. The NBA has been waiting for him to ascend to his rightful place as an MVP and champion. Both scenarios have been hard to come by and all Luka needs to do is look in the mirror.In my opinion, he runs a 24-mile marathon instead of 26.2 miles.”That metaphor set the tone. Johnson believes Doncic falls short in the final stretch, not because of talent, but because of habits.“Luka is so talented that he believes in shortcuts on the floor. That is not the personality for all-time greats. Gaining weight in-season, taking off defensive plays, arguing with officials and taking bad shots because you are tired. They all add up to failure and are the difference in winning what he needs, and that’s an MVP and a championship.”It is a harsh take, especially for a player who has already led a team to the NBA Finals and continues to post near triple-double averages. Yet the frustration behind it reflects a wider sentiment. Doncic has flirted with MVP contention before but has never fully seized it.The criticism lands at a crossroads in Doncic’s career. He is still one of the league’s most gifted scorers and playmakers, but expectations have evolved. Being great is no longer enough.There are signs of progress. Doncic entered the season in better shape and showed more effort on defense before injury slowed him down. His chemistry with LeBron James and Austin Reaves also hinted at a stronger structure in Los Angeles. Those improvements matter, even if they do not silence every critic.Still, Johnson’s words highlight a simple truth. The margin at the top of the NBA is thin. A few missed defensive rotations, a moment spent arguing instead of getting back, or a tired shot selection can tilt the balance. For Doncic, closing that gap could be the difference between being remembered as a great talent and becoming a complete winner.
Article Summary: Lakers guard Eddie Johnson criticizes Luka Doncic for not winning MVP. Johnson says the Slovenian needs to 'look in the mirror' and change his habits. The criticism lands at a crossroads in the 19-year-old's career.

Article Title: Is Mike Vrabel heading for divorce? Jennifer Vrabel’s silence after Dianna Russini controversy raises questions
Article Text:
Mike Vrabel is facing growing scrutiny after his link-up with Dianna Russini sparked speculation about his marriage to Jennifer Vrabel. While no official statements confirm issues, commentators suggest possible personal consequences. The Patriots continue business as usual, and Russini has resigned, but public attention remains fixed on Vrabel’s personal life and rumored strain at home.

Mike Vrabel divorce chatter grows louder amid fallout from Dianna Russini controversy

People are talking more and more about Mike Vrabel's personal life instead of his work on the field. After photos of the New England Patriots head coach with Dianna Russini surfaced online, attention quickly turned toward his marriage with Jennifer Vrabel. What might have been dismissed as a fleeting headline has now evolved into a deeper discussion about relationships, privacy, and public perception.The timing has made matters more intense. With the NFL Draft approaching, Vrabel would typically be focused on football decisions. Instead, the narrative around him feels split. On one side is his professional role, steady and unchanged. On the other is a wave of speculation about how the controversy may be affecting his life away from the game.The discussion gained traction after commentator Jason Whitlock shared his perspective on the situation. Speaking candidly, he suggested that the real consequences may not come from the league or the Patriots organization, but from within Vrabel’s own household."My contention is there will be consequences for Mike Vrabel by his primary employer, his wife. And she has a right to render consequences on him because his behavior impacted negatively their relationship."That statement has fueled ongoing speculation about the state of Vrabel’s marriage. While neither Vrabel nor his wife has publicly addressed the issue, observers have noted Jennifer’s low profile during recent team events. Still, there is no confirmed indication of any fallout beyond public discussion.The mood inside the Patriots organization is still calm. Eliot Wolf, a team executive, has said that Vrabel's interactions with Russini were only professional, which supports the idea that the situation is not affecting football operations. Vrabel is still a key part of the planning for the draft, which means that things are going on as usual behind the scenes.Russini has stepped down from her job at The Athletic while an internal review is going on, which is part of the larger story about her leaving. In her resignation, she stood firm, stating, "I have covered the NFL with professionalism and dedication throughout my career, and I stand behind every story I have ever published."Amid all the noise, one past comment from Vrabel has resurfaced, offering a different perspective on his marriage. Reflecting on his relationship, he once said, "I love her to death. She’s been through a lot, and she understands this league.”That line now carries more weight than ever. Whether the current situation changes anything remains unclear. For now, the story sits in that uncertain space between perception and reality, where headlines often move faster than facts.
Article Summary: New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel is facing growing scrutiny. His link-up with Dianna Russini sparked speculation about his marriage. No official statements confirm issues, but commentators suggest possible personal consequences. The Patriots continue business as usual, and Russini has resigned.

Article Title: Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes splurge $60,000 on a massive gift for their children as his recovery timeline sparks concern
Article Text:
Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes splurge $60,000 on a massive gift for their children as his recovery timeline sparks concern.(Image via Getty Images)

Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes make an expensive purchase for their family



When is Patrick Mahomes returning to the field amid his brutal injuries?



Patrick Mahomes, the Kansas City Chiefs’ star player, has been spending time recovering from his brutal injuries as he also enjoys the offseason being with his wife, Brittany Mahomes, and their three young children.Throughout the last few months, the Mahomes’ family has been pretty busy celebrating milestones and birthdays and it seems, Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes recently made a lavish purchase for their three children.A few hours ago, Patrick Mahomes’ wife, Brittany Mahomes, took to her social media to make a post about a massive custom built playhouse which the two bought for their backyard for their three young children.As per reports, the sprawling playhouse cost Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes a whopping $60,000.Patrick Mahomes’ wife, Brittany Mahomes, posted several pictures of their young children enjoying themselves in the playhouse, which fans have dubbed as a “mansion”.While the Kansas City Chiefs’ star player, Patrick Mahomes, is spending time playing with his children, all eyes are on whether he is ready to bounce back on the field in the next few months.A couple of weeks ago, the Kansas City Chiefs’ head coach, Andy Reid, opened up on Patrick Mahomes’ recovery at the NFL's annual league meetings as he said, "Every player is different. I will tell you though, I would never bet against him…”The popular coach continued, “He's going to put in the time and effort and always push it but within reason, so he doesn't take steps back.”Andy Reid added, "So far, it's all been positive. He's doing great."Over the last few months, there have been many speculations that Patrick Mahomes will be able to make a comeback in the first week of the new season.Back in January, the Kansas City Chiefs’ billionaire owner, Clark Hunt, had also commented on Patrick Mahomes’ recovery from ACL and LCL tears as he seemed assured that he will be able to make a return without missing any matches in the upcoming season.However, nothing has been confirmed yet as the team and the star player, Patrick Mahomes, remain silent on his potential return to the field after a poor run on the field in the last season.
Article Summary: Patrick Mahomes and his wife, Brittany Mahomes, recently splurged $60,000 on a massive playhouse for their three children. The Kansas City Chiefs’ star player has been recovering from ACL and LCL tears. There have been many speculations that Patrick Mahomes will be able to make a comeback in the first week of the new season.

Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Sooryavanshi termed next 'demigod' of cricket: Srikkanth invokes Tendulkar comparison
Article Text:
Sachin Tendulkar and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi

Watch Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation

‘No need to wait, he’s a match-winner’

Tendulkar comparison raises stakes

NEW DELHI: The buzz around Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has reached a fever pitch in IPL 2026, with former India captain Kris Srikkanth making a dramatic call to fast-track the 15-year-old sensation into the national setup — while even rival skipper Shreyas Iyer has labelled him “one for the future.”Srikkanth, known for his blunt takes, did not hold back as he urged selectors to act immediately. “This guy, Sooryavanshi… imagine someone playing such shots. That boy is simply outstanding and brilliant. Ajit, I’m telling you, you need to fast-track him. The next series, he should be playing,” he told The Week, addressing chief selector Ajit Agarkar The former chief selector dismissed the idea of a gradual approach, insisting the youngster is ready for the big stage. “I think Sooryavanshi should be fast-tracked. No question of ‘give him time, let him be consistent’. No chance. That guy can win matches for you just like that,” Srikkanth said.He pointed to the fearless manner in which the teenager has taken on elite bowlers this season — smashing Jasprit Bumrah for sixes and hammering Josh Hazlewood without hesitation. “Imagine, first ball, he hits Bumrah for a six… he’s not bothered about who the bowler is. He’s a prodigy,” he added.In a striking comparison, Srikkanth invoked Sachin Tendulkar ’s early rise. “When I went as captain in 1989, there was a prodigy — a 16-year-old Sachin Tendulkar. Today, he’s the God of Cricket. Similarly, this boy could well become the next ‘demigod’ of cricket,” he said.The excitement is not limited to former players. Punjab Kings skipper Shreyas Iyer also praised the youngster’s technique and temperament. “He is a spectacular talent… the way he has that bat flow, the rhythm and momentum… I think he is one for the future,” Iyer noted.Sooryavanshi has backed the hype with numbers, scoring 246 runs in six matches at a staggering strike rate of 236.53 this season.For now, all eyes remain on the prodigy as Rajasthan Royals face Lucknow Super Giants on Wednesday, with the teenager at the centre of Indian cricket’s next big debate.
Article Summary: Former India captain Kris Srikkanth has called for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to be fast-tracked into the national setup. Punjab Kings skipper Shreyas Iyer has labelled the 15-year-old “one for the future” The teenager has scored 246 runs in six matches at a staggering strike rate of 236.53 this season.

Article Title: London Nightclub Incident: Disturbing video surfaces as Klaudia Zakrzewska hit by Gabrielle Carrington Mercedes after clash outside London nightclub
Article Text:
Disturbing video surfaces as Klaudia Zakrzewska hit by Gabrielle Carrington Mercedes after clash outside London nightclub (Image via Getty)

Watch Klaudia Zakrzewska ‘Crushed’ In 4AM Clash; Soho Nightclub Horror Sparks Outrage | WATCH Klaudia Zakrzewska ‘Crushed’ In 4AM Clash; Soho Nightclub Horror Sparks Outrage | WATCH

Gabrielle Carrington and Klaudia Zakrzewska clash outside London nightclub turns violent







Klaudia Zakrzewska, known online as Klaudia Glam, is now fighting for her life after a shocking incident outside a London nightclub early Sunday morning. A disturbing video, shared on X, shows a heated fight turning into something far more serious. In the clip, another influencer, Gabrielle Carrington, 29, is seen arguing and physically clashing with Zakrzewska before getting into a black Mercedes. Moments later, the car moves toward Zakrzewska and hits her, leaving people around screaming in panic. One voice in the video clearly shouts, “She’s running her over!” as the situation quickly gets out of control. The car also hits two other people nearby. Reports say the vehicle then reversed over Zakrzewska, who was left lying still on the ground.Police have not confirmed her condition beyond saying she is in critical state. The incident happened around 4:30 a.m. outside Inca nightclub in Soho, Central London. The area was still busy at that hour, with many people around.According to police, Gabrielle Carrington has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder, grievous bodily harm, dangerous driving, and drink driving. Authorities believe she was the one driving the car seen in the video.She is known online as “rielleuk” and has over 360,000 Instagram followers.Klaudia Zakrzewska, who has around 260,000 followers under the name “klaudiaglam,” had been at the nightclub with friends before the fight began. Reports suggest the argument started inside and later moved outside, where things escalated quickly.Two other people were also injured during the incident. A man in his 50s, who was unlocking an e-scooter nearby, suffered serious, life-changing injuries and was taken to the hospital. A woman in her 30s had minor injuries and was treated at the scene.Gabrielle Carrington has a public social media presence where she often shares posts about luxury travel and designer items. In a post from April 7, she is seen standing next to a black Mercedes similar to the one involved in the crash, holding a Chanel handbag. Other posts show her traveling to places like Mexico, Jamaica, and France.Police are now asking anyone who saw what happened to come forward. Detective Chief Inspector Alixson Foxwell said, “While this incident took place in the early hours of the morning, venues in the area were still open, and we believe a number of people will have seen what happened.”She added, “I would urge anyone who witnessed the collision, or any activity prior to it that they feel may be of relevance, to come forward.” The investigation is ongoing, and officials are still gathering details from witnesses and video footage.
Article Summary: Klaudia Zakrzewska, known online as Klaudia Glam, is now fighting for her life after a shocking incident outside a London nightclub early Sunday morning. Gabrielle Carrington, 29, has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder, grievous bodily harm, dangerous driving, and drink driving. Police have not confirmed her condition beyond saying she is in critical state. Two other people were also injured during the incident.

Article Title: Jordon Hudson takes major step with Bill Belichick trademarks, leaving his family and friends deeply concerned
Article Text:
Jordon Hudson takes major step with Bill Belichick trademarks, leaving his family and friends deeply concerned (Image via Getty)

Jordon Hudson moves to secure Bill Belichick-linked trademarks as family concerns grow

Jordon Hudson is again at the center of attention, and this time it’s about money and control. The 25-year-old has taken a fresh step that could give her legal rights over names linked to Bill Belichick. According to WRAL, her company has moved forward with trademark filings tied to Belichick’s identity. This comes at a time when people close to the former NFL coach are already uneasy about how much influence she has in his life. Their relationship, which began in 2024, has faced constant scrutiny because of their 49-year age gap. Now, this new move is expected to make things even more tense within his inner circle.Hudson’s company, TCE Rights Management, has applied for several trademarks that clearly connect to Belichick’s name and image. The filings include “Chapel Bill” and “Chapel Bill (Bill’s version),” a play on Chapel Hill, where Belichick has been working recently. She has also applied for “All-Belichick Team” and “All-Time Belichick Team.”These filings have been published in the US Patent and Trademark Office’s Official Gazette. That starts a 30-day window where anyone can oppose them. If no one objects, the process will move forward to the next stage.This is not her first attempt. In 2025, her company filed at least 22 trademark applications, many linked to Belichick. Some were rejected, including phrases like “Do Your Job (Bill’s Version)” and “No Days Off (Bill’s Version),” which were earlier tied to the New England Patriots.People close to Belichick are not taking this lightly. As reported by The New York Post, some of his friends have already spoken to him about their worries. One source said they are being careful in how they approach him, but they are clearly concerned about how much say Hudson has in his decisions.The same report described Belichick, known for being firm and in control, as someone who now seems to be letting Hudson take the lead in many areas. That shift has raised eyebrows among those who have known him for years.There are also mixed views about Hudson from her own extended family, as reported by Daily Mail. Some relatives are not close to her and were surprised by her relationship with Belichick. However, she does appear to share a strong bond with her immediate family. Belichick was seen sitting with her father, Heath Hudson, during the Miss Maine USA pageant, showing support.Right now, the trademark situation is still developing. But one thing is clear, this step has added another layer to a relationship that was already under heavy public and private scrutiny.
Article Summary: Jordon Hudson moves to secure Bill Belichick-linked trademarks as family concerns grow. The 25-year-old has taken a fresh step that could give her legal rights over names linked to Belichick. This comes at a time when people close to the former NFL coach are already uneasy about how much influence she has.

Article Title: Yoram Zague sentenced to jail after Copenhagen speeding incident as PSG talent faces deportation scare and uncertain future
Article Text:
Yoram Zague (Image Via Getty)

Yoram Zague ’s high-speed case in Copenhagen puts his future at risk

Paris Saint-Germain youngster Zague is now facing serious trouble after a dangerous driving incident in Denmark. The 19-year-old has been sentenced to 20 days in jail and banned from driving for three years. The case has also raised concerns about his future in Europe, with authorities even warning about possible deportation.The incident happened while he was on loan at FC Copenhagen. Reports say he was driving at 65 mph on a bridge in Copenhagen where the speed limit was just 31 mph. The situation quickly turned serious, leading to his arrest in December and now a confirmed sentence. He has accepted the punishment and did not file any appeal.Yoram Zague’s loan spell from Paris Saint-Germain to FC Copenhagen was supposed to be a big step in his career. He joined last summer, and there was even an option for a permanent transfer. But things changed after his arrest.According to local reports and statements shared during the Frederiksberg trial, the defender admitted his mistake. He pleaded guilty and accepted the court’s decision. Prosecutor Josefine Samuelsen told local media, “Zague’s car has not been seized, due to the ownership situation around it.”Along with the jail term and driving ban, the court also warned that he could have been deported from Denmark. That warning alone shows how serious the situation was.Because of this incident, his deal with FC Copenhagen was ended early. He has now moved to KAS Eupen on another loan. However, he will have to return to Denmark to serve his 20-day sentence, which adds more uncertainty to his career.On the field, Zague had shown promise. He made nine appearances for PSG and even scored once against OGC Nice. During his time in Denmark, he scored two goals in 15 matches and was starting to build momentum.Now, everything is unclear. His time in Belgium could depend on how the club views this situation after he completes his sentence. What looked like a rising career has suddenly taken a difficult turn.
Article Summary: Yoram Zague has been sentenced to 20 days in jail and banned from driving for three years. The 19-year-old was driving at 65 mph on a bridge in Copenhagen where the speed limit was just 31 mph. The incident happened while he was on loan at FC Copenhagen. Authorities have warned that he could be deported from Denmark.

Article Title: Coco Gauff and Elena Rybakina practice twist grabs attention as Iga Swiatek joins Naomi Osaka before Madrid Open
Article Text:
Coco Gauff, Elena Rybakina, Iga Swiatek and Naomi Osaka (Image Via Getty)



Coco Gauff, Elena Rybakina, Iga Swiatek, and Naomi Osaka sharpen form ahead of Madrid Open matches

The courts in Madrid are already busy, even before the real matches begin. Big names like Coco Gauff, Elena Rybakina, Iga Swiatek, and Naomi Osaka have started their practice sessions as the Madrid Open main draw kicks off this week. Fans who came early got a close look at some strong pairings on the practice courts.Gauff was seen training with Rybakina, while Swiatek worked with Osaka. These sessions were not just light hits. Players were fully focused, working on details, fixing small mistakes, and getting ready for the long tournament ahead. Videos from the venue and social media clips showed the energy building up as the tournament officially begins.Gauff and Rybakina shared the court in a high-intensity session. Gauff spent time working on her volleys, listening closely to her coach and making quick adjustments. On the other side, Rybakina kept hitting powerful shots and even paused at times to talk through her technique after certain rallies. The crowd around the practice court stayed engaged throughout.At another court, Swiatek and Osaka trained together. One moment caught attention online — Swiatek arrived a bit late for the session, but quickly got into rhythm once practice started.The two former world No. 1 players looked sharp and focused.All four players will begin their campaigns in the Round of 64 on Thursday, with opponents still to be confirmed. Swiatek, who won Madrid in 2024, could face Aryna Sabalenka later in the tournament if both progress deep. Rybakina may open against either Elena-Gabriela Ruse or Antonia Ruzic and could meet Zheng Qinwen in the third round. Gauff, seeded third, might face Oksana Selekhmeteva or a qualifier, with a possible clash against Jessica Pegula in the later rounds.For Gauff, this tournament carries extra importance. She is still searching for her first singles title of the 2026 season. Her year has had good moments, like reaching the Australian Open quarter-final, but also tough losses. She was beaten by Elina Svitolina more than once, including in Dubai. She also suffered an early exit in Qatar against Elisabetta Cocciaretto.Things looked better in Miami, where she reached the final. But once again, Sabalenka proved too strong in the final match. Gauff also had a setback at Indian Wells, where she had to withdraw due to injury during her match against Alexandra Eala.Now on clay, she is hoping Madrid can change her season. Her first match in Stuttgart ended in a loss to Karolína Muchová, so she will want a better start here. The preparation looks serious, and the intent is clear.
Article Summary: Coco Gauff, Elena Rybakina, Iga Swiatek, and Naomi Osaka have started their practice sessions as the Madrid Open main draw kicks off this week. All four players will begin their campaigns in the Round of 64 on Thursday, with opponents still to be confirmed.

Article Title: Robot Wins Beijing Half Marathon: ‘Chinese Humanoid Robots’ beat ​human winners in half-marathon race, again ‘proving’ Elon Musk right about China being Tesla’s only competitor
Article Text:
Source: AP

How new robots better than last year’s machines

Elon Musk: ‘Tesla’s only real competitor is China’

Poll Do you believe humanoid robots will surpass human athletes in the near future? Yes, definitely No, humans will always be better





In what can be called a display of athleticism by machines, dozens of Chinese-made humanoid robots ran past professional runners in a half-marathon in Beijing on Sunday (April 19). The performance was dominant as they not only broke last year’s record but also surpassed human runners to reach the finish line, seemingly confirming Elon Musk’s prediction that China is Tesla’s most formidable competitor in the AI and robotics space.According to a report by news agency Reuters, the event showed how fast technology in China is moving. A year ago, the same race was filled with mishaps and slow-moving machines, and the contrast between this year and last was “stark”. The report noted that while last year's champion robot took over two hours and 40 minutes to finish, this year’s winner crossed the finish line in 50 minutes and 26 seconds.To put in perspective how better the current generation of robots are, the winner robot, developed by the Chinese brand Honor, was several minutes faster than the human world record (57:31) set by Jacob Kiplimo in Lisbon.Unlike previous years where robots were remote-controlled, nearly half of this year’s entrants navigated the 21 km course entirely on their own using AI.Engineers explained that the winning robot was designed to mimic elite human runners, featuring legs roughly 36 inches long.The team borrowed “liquid cooling” technology to keep the robot’s motors cool during the intense physical exertion. The same tech is used to keep smartphones cooler during extended period of usage. Engineers say the real goal by making the robots run half-marathon is to prove that these robots are durable enough for future work in factories and manufacturing.The race results serve as a reminder of comments made by Tesla CEO Elon Musk. During an earnings call last year, Musk noted that China would likely be Tesla’s biggest rival in the humanoid market.Musk said, “I think China will be by far the biggest competitor in the humanoid robot market. China is extremely good at scaling and manufacturing and is also strong in AI—the models being released there are already quite good and are improving rapidly.”Earlier this year, China’s annual CCTV Spring Festival Gala, often compared to the US Super Bowl, showcased humanoid robots from four domestic startups during the Lunar New Year celebrations this week. ThisThe gala featured robots from Unitree Robotics, Galbot, Noetix, and MagicLab performing martial arts and fight sequences with swords, poles, and nunchucks alongside human child performers.
Article Summary: Chinese-made humanoid robots ran past professional runners in a half-marathon in Beijing on Sunday (April 19) The performance was dominant as they not only broke last year’s record but also surpassed human runners to reach the finish line. The winner robot, developed by the Chinese brand Honor, was several minutes faster than the human world record (57:31)

Article Title: G Parameshwara: Bengaluru court orders FIR against home minister G Parameshwara over Rs 500 betting remark
Article Text:
G Parameshwara

BENGALURU: A Bengaluru court has ordered registration of an FIR against home minister G Parameshwara over an alleged betting remark.At a recent state-level PU kabaddi tournament he-ld in Tumakuru, Dakshina Kannada defeated Vijayapura 36-26 in the boys' final.After presenting the prize, Parameshwara jokingly said, "I lost Rs 500-I had placed a bet with the DC that the Vijayapura team would win."The 42nd additional chief judicial magistrate court directed police to initiate a probe following a private complaint filed by H R Nagabhushan.The court also instructed Kodigehalli police in Tumakuru to conduct a detailed investigation.

Article Title: Pahalgam Terror: Pahalgam massacre widowed her, but Bengaluru doctor fights on for her 4-year-old son
Article Text:
Bharat Bhushan & Dr Sujatha

Watch Indian Army Sends Strong Warning to Terror Groups Ahead of Pahalgam Terror Attack Anniversary Indian Army Sends Strong Warning to Terror Groups Ahead of Pahalgam Terror Attack Anniversary

Attackers brainwashed to believe what they do is right: Dr Sujatha

BENGALURU: A year after the Pahalgam terror attack that claimed the life of her husband, Bharath Bhushan, Dr Sujatha still measures time in 'before' and 'after'. What remains, she says, is a void. "I've lost my emotion... I've lost my smile," she says quietly.Bharath Bhushan, an MBA graduate, complemented her life as a doctor. "He knew the world. I knew my patients. We balanced each other. Now, I don't have my emotional pillar."Her four-and-a-half-year-old son, who witnessed the attack, has responded in ways that both comfort and concern her. "He saw his father being shot. We were both splattered with blood," she recalls. "He kept saying, 'Papa is hurt, there's so much blood.'"Bharath Bhushan's photograph sits beside Dr Sujatha in her clinic, a silent presence that she says guides her decisions. But the attack casts a long shadow. "To cope, I keep myself busy from morning till night. If I'm idle, my thoughts drift back to that scene," she said. "That moment weighs more than all the happiness we shared."Despite long working hours across hospitals, and freelance consultations, Sundays are reserved for her son."I consciously kept Sundays free. He needs me."Since that day, the child has not once asked for his father. "He's very calm, like his father. He doesn't throw tantrums when I'm not around. Only with me, when I'm home," she says, with a faint smile. "I'm thankful he's such a well-behaved child."Gradually, she has helped him understand the loss. "He knows his father is no more. I keep engaging him, playing, and teaching. My family supports me a lot."April, once a month of celebration, is now one of grief. The family had just celebrated Bharath Bhushan's birthday days before the attack. "We were planning a family outing on April 14. We always visited a temple first and then went out. Now, this month is something I can never forget."Having heard from her parents about heavy security presence in Kashmir, Dr Sujatha says she was not too worried when she first heard the noise. "At first, we thought it was crackers. Then, I thought security personnel would handle it but there was no security in that area. Not even one person. If there had been even a single armed guard, this could have been avoided."She also points to a deeper issue: radicalisation. "These attackers are brainwashed to believe what they're doing is right. Something needs to change. I don't know how, but this cannot continue."Since the attack, fear has become a constant companion. "I'm always alert, looking around, even while driving. Not ahead, but at trees, and corners, wondering if someone is targeting us," she told TOI.When asked about revisiting a memory with her husband, she says it would be their wedding day. "I was so happy. I kept asking him, 'How could you choose me?'" They had been married for six years when Pahalgam happened. "I've pushed myself to keep going," she says. "But the loss, it's always there."
Article Summary: A year after the Pahalgam terror attack, Dr Sujatha still measures time in 'before' and 'after' Her four-and-a-half-year-old son, who witnessed the attack, has responded in ways that both comfort and concern her. "These attackers are brainwashed to believe what they're doing is right," she says.

Article Title: Lucas Erceg: Lucas Erceg's net worth in 2026: From alcohol struggles to MLB closer, how his life and salary tell a powerful story
Article Text:
Lucas

Lucas Erceg's net worth and salary details



Lucas Erceg's early life and struggles before MLB success

Lucas Erceg's personal life and turning point

Lucas Erceg's MLB career and recent 2026 update

Lucas Erceg is not just another baseball player chasing money. His story feels real because it’s about struggle, mistakes, and slowly getting his life back on track. He has seen tough days, both on and off the field, but things started to change when he made some big personal decisions. By 2026, his journey shows how far he has come, not just as a player, but as a person too.Lucas Erceg’s estimated net worth is about $1.67 million (According to Salary Sport). This number comes from sports salary tracking websites that collect player contract data.His yearly MLB salary is reported to be around $720,000. His family issues created obstacles which he overcame through his dedication to baseball. He played at Westmont High School and later joined the University of California, Berkeley.There, he performed well and even earned All-Pac-12 honors in 2015.However, he also struggled with alcohol during this time, which affected his studies. He later moved to Menlo College to continue both his education and baseball journey.Erceg’s life changed in 2020. After years of struggling with alcohol, he decided to quit drinking on June 10, 2020. This decision came after support from his partner, Emma, who is now his wife.He has maintained his sobriety while pursuing his professional ambitions since that time. The couple got married and welcomed their daughter in December 2025.The personal transformation which he experienced at that time enabled him to maintain his strength during games and outside the stadium.Erceg made his MLB debut in 2023 with the Oakland Athletics. He joined the Kansas City Royals bullpen after his time with the Oakland Athletics ended because he became an essential member of their relief team.He took over as closer in early 2026 after the team placed Carlos Estévez on the injured list. Article Title: Ipl 2026: IPL 2026: Will DC make changes to XI after defeat to SRH? Skipper Axar Patel clarifies
Article Text:
SRH vs DC

Watch Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation

NEW DELHI: Sunrisers Hyderabad’s dominant win over Delhi Capitals was once again defined by Abhishek Sharma ’s explosive batting and a disciplined bowling effort led by Eshan Malinga. However, DC skipper Axar Patel remained calm after the defeat, suggesting no immediate panic or changes to the XI despite the heavy loss.Speaking after the match, Axar backed his squad and stressed that the team’s overall process was still on track. He said, "No, right now it doesn’t feel like that, because the kind of cricket that is going on - if you see, obviously we are batting well and bowling well. It feels like, I think you know, when the wicket is so good, then this should be considered an off day and forgotten."He also acknowledged missed opportunities in the field that hurt DC’s chances against Abhishek’s onslaught. "The run-out and the catch we missed - had we taken it, we could have restricted them," he said, while still giving full credit to SRH’s batting firepower.Axar further added that even good execution sometimes fails against extraordinary batting. "If someone bats that well, and their execution is not lacking, credit must be given for such a performance... when a batsman bats like this, the coach and the captain must accept that even a captain cannot stop someone," he explained.He also emphasised the importance of support within the team environment during tough games, particularly for bowlers under pressure.The match itself saw Abhishek Sharma produce a breathtaking unbeaten 135 off 68 balls, powering SRH to a massive 242/2. Despite a brief fightback from Delhi’s middle order, Malinga’s 4/32 broke the chase apart as DC were restricted to 195/9.Abhishek’s knock, supported by Travis Head and Heinrich Klaasen, ensured SRH’s dominance from the start, while Axar admitted that sometimes, opponents simply outplay you on the day.
Article Summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Delhi Capitals by eight wickets. Abhishek Sharma top scored for SRH with 135 off 68 balls. Eshan Malinga took 4/32 as DC were restricted to 195/9.

Article Title: Yogi Adityanath: UP CM Yogi Adityanath mixes up Subhas Chandra Bose's ‘Give me blood, I will give you freedom' to Swami Vivekananda, draws flak
Article Text:
Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath's misattribution of Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose's famous "give me blood, I will give you freedom" slogan to Swami Vivekananda has sparked widespread criticism.

Watch Mamata Alleges Flight Delay, Administrative Bias And Political Interference | Bengal Polls Mamata Alleges Flight Delay, Administrative Bias And Political Interference | Bengal Polls

KOLKATA: Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath 's faux pas on Monday attributing Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose's iconic "give me blood, I will give you freedom" slogan to Swami Vivekananda led to sharp criticism not only from Trinamool but also from Samajwadi Party in his home state.Speaking at a campaign rally in Bankura's Joypur, Adityanath said, "Swami Vivekananda said, ‘give me blood, I will give you freedom'."Trinamool MP Sagarika Ghose was the first to react. In a post on X, she said: "The utter and shameful illiteracy of BJP on Bengal is exposed every day again and again. No Yogi Adityanath Ji, it was NOT Swami Vivekananda but Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose who uttered the iconic slogan. Give me blood, I promise you freedom. Go home BJP. Don't even try in Bengal."Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav reacted. Sharing a video of the incident, Yadav said Adityanath's comment "has dealt a deep blow to Uttar Pradesh's image across the entire world — people are saying, if the reins of UP are in the hands of such folk, then what hope is there?""That said, one reason for this could be that people from their faction weren't even part of the freedom struggle, so how would these underground role-players — who only pass information from one ear to the other — know the history of freedom fighters? They've always been the sort who let knowledge go in one ear and out the other," Yadav added.Adityanath has repeatedly referred to Bengal icons such as Rabindranath Tagore, Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay, Ramakrishna Paramhansa in his speeches.Trinamool, in a post on X, said the comment had exposed "BJP's shocking ignorance and contempt for Bengal's history." The party further said that the UP CM insulted two sons of the soil in one sentence.The slogan in question, "give me blood, I will give you freedom" was raised by Netaji during his July 1944 declaration in Burma to Indian National Army (INA) soldiers to fight against British rule.
Article Summary: Yogi Adityanath's misattribution of Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose's famous "give me blood, I will give you freedom" slogan to Swami Vivekananda has sparked widespread criticism. Trinamool MP Sagarika Ghose: "The utter and shameful illiteracy of BJP on Bengal is exposed every day again and again" Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav: "People are saying... what hope is there?"

Article Title: Hyderabad Techie Arrest: Hyderabad techie loses job, turns to cultivation of ganja; held
Article Text:
A software engineer in Hyderabad was arrested for cultivating ganja plants at his residence after losing his job due to substance addiction.

By Manvi Vyas

HYDERABAD: A software engineer, who allegedly lost his job due to substance addiction, was arrested on Tuesday for growing ganja plants at his house in East Gandhinagar in Nacharam.Excise officials said the accused, Ch Shashidhar, 37, was habituated to ganja and alcohol. Acting on a tip-off, the Ranga Reddy Enforcement ‘A' team conducted a raid at the premises and seized 17 ganja plants. Officials said the plants grew up to three metres and some of them even began flowering. The yield from the plants would be about 10 kg, they said."He started spending most of what he earned to procure the contraband and was eventually terminated from his job. To cater to his personal needs after the loss of employment, he began growing ganja plants on the terrace of his house," an excise official said.Ghatkesar excise sub-inspector Sangeetha Reddy said: "He would extract the seeds from ganja he used to buy and began growing the contraband on his terrace."The accused was handed over to the Ghatkesar excise station officials for further action.Two BTech students arrestedIn a related development, the same excise team arrested two BTech students in Medipally for selling ganja. The accused, Bandi Arun Kumar and B Ashish, were found in possession of 480gm of the substance. The two accused were also handed over to Ghatkesar excise officials.
Article Summary: Excise officials said the accused, Ch Shashidhar, 37, was habituated to ganja and alcohol. Acting on a tip-off, the Ranga Reddy Enforcement ‘A' team conducted a raid at the premises. The plants grew up to three metres and some even began flowering.

Article Title: Hailee Steinfeld shares heartbreaking confession after welcoming baby with Josh Allen
Article Text:
Hailee Steinfeld, now a new mother with Josh Allen, shared that she deeply misses creating music videos, calling them a blend of her love for music and film. While embracing motherhood, she admitted the music industry no longer feels as creatively fulfilling. Still, she often thinks about returning to music, leaving the door open for a future comeback when the timing feels right.

Hailee Steinfeld opens up about missing music videos amid new chapter

Poll Do you believe the music industry undervalues music videos today? Yes, definitely! No, they have their place

The spotlight around Josh Allen and Hailee Steinfeld has shifted in recent months. Life now revolves around their newborn daughter, a change that has quietly reshaped Steinfeld’s daily rhythm. While fans often see the polished public version of her life, the actress-singer has been candid about what she has had to set aside, at least for now. Her latest reflections offer a rare, personal look into the trade-offs that come with motherhood.Through her “Beau Society” newsletter, Hailee Steinfeld peeled back another layer. She did not dwell on sleepless nights or routines. Instead, she spoke about something more emotional. It is the creative itch that has stayed with her, even as priorities evolve. That honesty has resonated, especially with fans who have followed her journey from teenage breakout star to a multifaceted performer balancing fame and family.In her recent newsletter entry, Josh Allen's wife Hailee Steinfeld did not hold back when describing the part of her career she longs for most. “Boy, do I miss making music videos,” she says in the post, with two exclamation marks. “I’ve always seen them as these perfect mini movies that combine my two passions: music and film. As an artist, I care deeply about every aspect of telling a story.”That passion goes beyond just remembering. Steinfeld never thought of music videos as just ways to sell her music.They were parts of who she was as a singer and an actress. She even thought about how chaotic things were behind the scenes and said that making them often meant "making six different outfit changes in a tiny trailer parked in the middle of nowhere." Still, those busy times seem to be very important to her.Steinfeld's comments also revealed the tension between artistry and industry realities. “Execs at my label would tell me, ‘No one cares about music videos anymore,'” she added. “I quickly gathered what they were really saying: There’s no budget. Well, there’s always budget… and I’ve always cared! I still care today — so many people do.”For now, music remains on pause. Steinfeld has acknowledged she thinks about returning, though the industry no longer feels as creatively fulfilling as it once did. Yet her words suggest the door is far from closed. Between motherhood and evolving ambitions, she appears to be waiting for the right moment when inspiration and timing finally align again.
Article Summary: Hailee Steinfeld, now a new mother with Josh Allen, shared that she deeply misses creating music videos. While embracing motherhood, she admitted the music industry no longer feels as creatively fulfilling. Still, she often thinks about returning to music, leaving the door open for a future comeback.

Article Title: “He would’ve killed me”- Andrew Tate Misfits Boxing star Deen The Great accused of brutal assault as his cheap shot leaves therapist bloodied
Article Text:
Deen The Great (via Getty)

Therapist describes injuries and claims Deen The Great's surprise attack



A therapist has spoken out after a shocking incident involving Andrew Tate's Misfits Boxing champion Deen The Great went viral online. The altercation reportedly left the therapist with serious facial injuries. Images and videos shared on social media showed visible swelling and damage.The injured individual later posted a video. He explained his condition and the pain he is going through. He claimed that the punch came unexpectedly and that he did not have a chance to defend himself. The incident has raised concerns about safety and accountability, especially when trained fighters are involved in public altercations. Many users are now debating whether legal action should be taken.The therapist gave a detailed account of his injuries in a video shared online. He explained that the damage to his face is real and severe. He said he is currently dealing with a broken nose, facial fractures, and a chipped tooth. He also mentioned that he needed stitches and is still in pain while speaking. According to him, one of his eyes is almost completely shut, and he is struggling to see clearly. He added that even basic movements, like trying to open his eyes, are difficult and painful.He further explained that he was not acting aggressively during the encounter. He said he was calm and even told the Deen the Great that he works as a therapist. He claimed that the punch came suddenly, without warning, and caught him off guard. Because of that, he did not react or fight back. He emphasized that facing a trained boxer in such a situation would have been dangerous, and he believes the outcome could have been much worse.The therapist also addressed online criticism. Some people questioned why he did not defend himself. In response, he explained that he was unprepared. He said he was not expecting violence. He stressed that the boxer’s professional training made the punch extremely powerful. He also hinted at the possibility of taking legal action but admitted that financial limitations could make it difficult.Meanwhile, Crip Mac also spoke about his own experience with Deen the Great in a separate interview. He admitted that he was knocked out cleanly during their exchange. He praised Deen the Great's punching ability and said that once the hit landed, he immediately felt the impact and knew he was going down. He acknowledged that trained boxers are highly skilled and dangerous in close combat situations.
Article Summary: Therapist describes injuries and claims Deen The Great's surprise attack caught him off guard. He said he is currently dealing with a broken nose, facial fractures, and a chipped tooth. Crip Mac also spoke about his own experience with Deen the Great in a separate interview.

Article Title: Tiger Woods son Charlie Woods struggles continue with T42 at Terra Cotta Invitational 2026 as Justin Thomas steps in with support
Article Text:
Charlie Woods (Image Via Getty)

Charlie Woods struggles continue despite late recovery at Terra Cotta Invitational as Justin Thomas offers support

Watch Tiger Woods DUI Bodycam Footage Caught SOS Call To Trump; Shocking Crash & Pills Reveal Chaos Tiger Woods DUI Bodycam Footage Caught SOS Call To Trump; Shocking Crash & Pills Reveal Chaos

The latest outing for Charlie Woods once again showed a mix of hope and struggle. Playing at the Terra Cotta Invitational 2026 at Naples National Golf Club, he finished tied 42nd in a field of 72 players. His total score of 3 over par came from rounds of 79, 71, and 69. On paper, it looks like progress. But when you follow the round closely, it tells a more uneven story.What stands out is how his performance changed day by day. The first round put him on the back foot early, and from there, he kept trying to recover. While the last two rounds showed better control, the slow start cost him a strong finish. This pattern is becoming familiar in his recent tournaments, where a few good moments are not enough to balance the mistakes.The opening round made things tough right away. Starting from the back nine, Tiger's son Charlie Woods dropped three straight bogeys from holes 16 to 18. That early pressure stayed with him. He added more bogeys on holes 2, 5, 6, and 8. There were birdies on 4 and 9, but they could not fully fix the damage. By the end of day one, he was near the bottom of the leaderboard.Then came a better second round. He shot 1 under 71, mixing four bogeys with five birdies.That round helped him move up from 70th to 57th. It showed he could reset and fight back. The final round was even better. After a bogey on the first hole, he settled down and picked up birdies on 6, 7, 9, and 18. That pushed him up 15 spots to finish T42.Still, this is not just about one event. His 2026 season has been difficult overall. At the Junior Invitational, he finished last with rounds of 75, 76, 83, and 80. At the AJGA Simplify Boys Championship, he placed 68th. He did slightly better at the Junior Orange Bowl, finishing 19th, but consistency is still missing.Even with these results, there is support around him. Justin Thomas spoke about him on the Smylie Show. He said, “I wanna be if he wants me to be. I wanna help in any way I can. The shots that he can hit, a lot of guys on Tour can’t hit. He’s very, very impressive and I’m rooting for the best.” Justin Thomas shares a close bond with Tiger Woods, which makes his support feel more personal.Right now, Tiger's son Charlie Woods is still finding his rhythm. The talent is visible, but the results are not steady yet. Until he can put together full rounds without mistakes, these mixed finishes are likely to continue.
Article Summary: Charlie Woods finished tied 42nd at the Terra Cotta Invitational 2026. The first round put him on the back foot early, and from there, he kept trying to recover. This pattern is becoming familiar in his recent tournaments, where a few good moments are not enough to balance the mistakes.

Article Title: Connor Mcdavid: Connor McDavid’s wife Lauren Kyle celebrates emotional milestone as Oilers captain gears up for electrifying Stanley Cup playoff run
Article Text:
Connor McDavid’s wife (Getty Images)

Connor McDavid’s wife reacts to milestone ahead of Oilers’ high-stakes playoff campaign

Poll What will be the biggest factor for the Oilers in their playoff matchups? Player health and fitness Coaching strategies Overall team morale

The Edmonton Oilers closed their regular season with purpose, delivering a 6-1 win over the Vancouver Canucks that did more than pad the standings. It set a tone. At the center of it, Connor McDavid dictated play with four assists, wrapping up another elite campaign. The result secured second place in the Pacific Division and home-ice advantage for the opening round. Off the ice, the moment carried a personal note as Lauren Kyle shared her pride publicly.Connor McDavid’s final outing wasn’t just productive. It felt controlled, almost routine for a player operating at this level. His four assists helped power an early surge, including a standout opening period where Matt Savoie recorded his first NHL hat trick. The Oilers never looked back from there.After the game, McDavid didn’t overstate it, but he made it clear the win mattered beyond the scoreboard. “It was an important one. We wanted to start here at home and give ourselves the best chance in the first round. The start was great,” he said.That clarity extended to his line’s development. There has been a noticeable shift in chemistry, especially with Savoie and Zach Hyman alongside him. “(Me and Savoie are) building something a little bit, playing together for a little while now.We’re starting to understand each other a little bit. (Zach Hyman) came in and did exactly what he does, forechecks hard and fills that net-front spot, which frees up a lot of space for us. ”Numbers back up the impact. McDavid secured the Art Ross Trophy with 138 points, his sixth time leading the league. That places him alongside Mario Lemieux and Gordie Howe, trailing only Wayne Gretzky in all-time scoring titles. It’s rare company, and it adds weight to what already feels like a defining stretch in his career.Away from the numbers, Lauren Kyle’s posts from Rogers Place gave a glimpse into the moment. A shot of the scoreboard came with a simple line, “Off to the playoffs!!” Another message, just as direct, read, “So proud of you.” It wasn’t elaborate, but it didn’t need to be. The context spoke for itself.Edmonton’s wider performance also offered encouragement. Ryan Nugent-Hopkins contributed offensively, while Colton Dach added a Gordie Howe hat trick. Cleaner puck movement and sharper finishing stood out, areas that had wavered at times earlier in the schedule.The focus now shifts quickly. A first-round matchup with the Anaheim Ducks begins April 20. McDavid’s form is a given at this point. The question is whether the rhythm building around him holds under playoff pressure.
Article Summary: Edmonton Oilers beat Vancouver Canucks 6-1 to clinch second place in the Pacific Division. Connor McDavid had four assists in the win, his sixth Art Ross Trophy. His wife, Lauren Kyle, posted a message to her husband from Rogers Place.

Article Title: Aryna Sabalenka fiancé Georgios Frangulis addresses critics questioning his presence during her tennis matches
Article Text:
Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis (Image Via Getty)

Money Buys Happiness podcast



Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis relationship and response to criticism explained

Esquire

There has been a lot of talk around Aryna Sabalenka and her fiancé Georgios Frangulis. Many fans have noticed him sitting in the stands during her matches, and not everyone has been supportive. Some people even questioned how he manages his business while attending so many games. Now, Georgios has finally responded, and his message was very direct.In a video shared by theon Instagram, Georgios made it clear that he is not bothered by the criticism. He spoke openly about his relationship and how they support each other. At the same time, he also pushed back at people who keep judging his choices and his presence during Sabalenka’s matches.Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis started dating in 2024, and earlier this year, they announced their engagement. Since then, Georgios has often been seen in her player box during big matches. While many fans enjoy seeing that support, others have raised questions about his work and time.Georgios Frangulis addressed this directly in his statement. He said, “For me to have someone that I love that trusts me and looks up to me.I can teach and learn from, with my fiancé. With Aryna it’s all upside. I don’t see any downside.”He then spoke about the criticism he receives and how he handles his work. He explained that watching matches does not stop him from managing his responsibilities. He said, “Believe me I get criticism. How can he run the company. He’s always watching tennis. You see me on TV and I am watching tennis. It’s a two-hour match. Like I was doing a meeting before I’m gonna do another after. Not your business what I am doing.”Georgios Frangulis also praised Aryna Sabalenka’s success and their shared understanding. He added, “She’s super accomplished. We can enjoy each other’s success, help each other’s failures.”On the other side, Sabalenka has also spoken about how important this relationship is for her. In an interview with, she shared a simple lesson she has learned. She said, “You see that each area of life takes the same dedication, discipline, and hard work.”While all this is happening off the court, Sabalenka’s form on the court has been strong. She started the season by winning the Brisbane International and then reached the final of the Australian Open. She later completed the Sunshine Double by winning both Indian Wells and the Miami Open, beating top players like Elena Rybakina and Coco Gauff.She was set to play at the Stuttgart Open but pulled out due to an injury picked up in Miami. Even so, both her performance and her personal life continue to stay in the spotlight.
Article Summary: Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis started dating in 2024, and earlier this year, they announced their engagement. While many fans enjoy seeing that support, others have raised questions about his work and time. He said, “For me to have someone that I love that trusts me and looks up to me, with my fiancé it’s all upside.”

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Cleveland Browns and Dallas Cowboys linked to bold first-round blockbuster swap
Article Text:
The Cleveland Browns are rumored to explore a blockbuster first-round trade with the Dallas Cowboys, potentially turning one top pick into three. Draft analyst Mel Kiper Jr. suggests Cleveland could address multiple needs, including offense and secondary depth. GM Andrew Berry remains flexible, emphasizing asset maximization as the Browns weigh several draft scenarios ahead of a pivotal night.

Browns and Cowboys could pull off stunning Round 1 trade that reshapes NFL draft

The Cleveland Browns may be setting up one of the most aggressive draft-night plays in recent memory, and it could come at the expense of the Dallas Cowboys. With two first-round picks already in hand, Cleveland has both flexibility and leverage. Now, a bold projection from Mel Kiper Jr. suggests the front office might lean all the way in.That scenario hinges on a swap that would send Cleveland down from No. 6 while stacking even more premium capital. It is not just about moving picks. It is about reshaping the roster in one night. And if executed, it could quietly redefine how the Browns approach contention in a loaded AFC.“Let’s have some fun here. Dallas Cowboys and Cleveland Browns each have two first-round picks coming into the draft, which gives those front offices some ability to maneuver. With an elite prospect still on the board, I have Cowboys owner Jerry Jones calling up Browns general manager Andrew Berry to try to make something happen,” Kiper said.“In this scenario, Dallas would trade pick Nos. 12 and 20 to Cleveland for Nos. 6 and 39. That means the Browns suddenly have three first-round picks.”If that unfolds, Cleveland Browns could attack multiple weaknesses at once. Offensive tackle Kadyn Proctor would bring protection and long-term stability.Wide receiver Denzel Boston adds a vertical threat. Then comes the curveball. Cornerback Jermod McCoy, a talented but recovering prospect, could round out the trio.“Proctor and Boston would tick the two biggest need boxes, and finding McCoy — one of the top cornerback prospects in the class — at No. 24 would be the cherry on top,” Kiper said.“Of course, McCoy hasn’t played a football game since 2024; he tore his ACL in January 2025 and sat out all of last season. But the tape is really good, and his ball skills (six interceptions in two seasons at Oregon State and Tennessee) are tremendous. Denzel Ward, Tyson Campbell and McCoy would form a solid trio at the position.”General manager Andrew Berry has made it clear the Browns are not locked into one path.“Our mindset going into the draft with our most valuable asset isn’t about, ‘hey, just trade it away.’ It’s maximizing the asset. And that can, at different times, take different forms. It can be selecting a player,” Berry said.“It could be trading it for a veteran; it could be trading down; it could be trading up. We will continue to work through all those possibilities up until we get on the clock on Thursday night. … We’re working through a number of different scenarios at this point.”Cleveland Browns has pulled off bold moves before. This time, the stakes feel even higher.
Article Summary: Cleveland Browns and Dallas Cowboys each have two first-round picks coming into the draft. Draft analyst Mel Kiper Jr. suggests Cleveland could address multiple needs, including offense and secondary depth. General manager Andrew Berry has made it clear the Browns are not locked into one path.

Article Title: Jim Rutherford sparks NHL trade rumors over Quinn Hughes' Vancouver Canucks exit raising Minnesota Wild absence concerns
Article Text:
Quinn Hughes, who recently joined the Minnesota Wild, was absent from multiple practice sessions due to his illness( Image via Imagn Images)

Jim Rutherford explains Quinn Hughes of the Vancouver Canucks NHL trade rumors

Jim Rutherford's comments about Quinn Hughes' departure have put the Vancouver Canucks back in the news, sparking a lot of NHL trade rumors. His comments made it seem like the star defenseman's departure was planned, which took the focus off the team's performance and put it on the player's intentions.Hughes not being with the Minnesota Wild before Game 1 has also made things more uncertain. When you add in the earlier rumors about the New Jersey Devils making a trade, this situation has quickly become a big story in the NHL. Jim Rutherford explains Quinn Hughes’ Vancouver Canucks exit by stating the player had already decided to leave. The Vancouver Canucks executive emphasized that Hughes’ move was not entirely tied to team struggles but rather a personal decision to eventually return to the United States.This point of view has sparked NHL trade rumors because it changes the way people talk about player movement and free agency. Rutherford said that this was like other NHL situations where players picked their favorite teams, no matter how competitive they were.These comments are important because they come at a time when the Canucks are still dealing with changes in their organization. It also makes us think about how teams keep their best players in a league where players are moving around more and more.Quinn Hughes' current situation with the Minnesota Wild has made the talks even more heated. The defenseman missed practice before Game 1, and reports say that illness was the main reason.Adding to the mystery, earlier NHL trade rumors linked Hughes to the New Jersey Devils, where there was a lot of talk about him getting back together with his brothers.Hughes' availability could have a big impact on how the Minnesota Wild set up their defense, at least from the point of view of an NHL match preview.Rutherford's comments may have made one part of Hughes' leaving clearer. But they have also led to more talk about how players can make their own choices and how teams should plan their strategies in the modern NHL, leaving front offices facing tougher challenges in retaining stars amid evolving player priorities.The situation also highlights how communication from front offices can shape public perception almost instantly, especially in today’s fast-moving digital media environment .
Article Summary: Jim Rutherford's comments about Quinn Hughes' departure have put the Vancouver Canucks back in the news. His comments made it seem like the star defenseman's departure was planned, which took the focus off the team's performance and put it on the player's intentions. Hughes not being with the Minnesota Wild before Game 1 has also made things more uncertain.

Article Text:

Matthew Knies of Toronto Maple Leafs linked to Chicago Blackhawks trade scenario

Matthew Knies, a forward for the Toronto Maple Leafs, is once again at the center of NHL trade rumors. Several teams are interested in him. As teams figure out their offseason priorities and roster needs, things are changing quickly.People are saying that the Chicago Blackhawks might trade for Matthew Knies to make their forward lineup stronger. Sources close to the NHL say that Chicago is looking into ways to add a tough, goal-scoring winger who can help their young core.The Blackhawks are interested in this because they need to build around young players like Connor Bedard. Adding someone like Knies could have an immediate effect and be useful in the long run. Even though Chicago is rebuilding, they have looked into adding established young players in the past instead of just relying on draft picks.From Toronto's point of view, it wouldn't be easy to move Knies. The 23-year-old has made a lot of progress and is seen as a key part of the team's future.If the Maple Leafs do want to change their roster, they might want to look at offers that include high draft picks or top prospects.The most important question is still what kind of return Toronto would want. The asking price could be high because Knies is young, strong, and has a lot of potential to score. On the other hand, Chicago needs to decide if it's worth giving up valuable things to speed up the rebuilding process.This possible situation is part of a bigger trend in the NHL: teams trying to plan for the future while still being competitive right now. In the next NHL updates, pay attention to the link between Toronto and Chicago, whether or not a deal goes through, as trade talks intensify and franchises weigh short-term gains against long-term rebuilding goals.Until then, Knies remains an important piece in Toronto’s lineup and a name to watch closely in ongoing NHL trade chatter, with speculation expected to grow as offseason decisions approach rapidly.
